faculty.name: Rana X. Adhikari
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental gravity and gravitational-wave detection. LIGO scientific collaboration. Quantum noise reduction in interferometric detectors.

faculty.name: Jason F. Alicea
faculty.title: William K. Davis Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physics. Topological phases of matter, Majorana fermions, and topological quantum computation.

faculty.name: Marco Bernardi
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics, Physics and Materials Science
faculty.summary: Theory and ab initio computation of light-matter interaction, carrier dynamics, and transport in materials.

faculty.name: James J. (Jamie) Bock
faculty.title: Marvin L. Goldberger Professor of Physics; JPL Senior Research Scientist
faculty.summary: Experimental cosmology. CMB polarization measurements searching for inflationary gravitational waves. BICEP/Keck and SPIDER experiments.

faculty.name: Fernando Brandao
faculty.title: Bren Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Quantum information science. Entanglement theory, quantum algorithms, and connections between quantum information and many-body physics.

faculty.name: Katerina Chatziioannou
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; William H. Hurt Scholar
faculty.summary: Gravitational-wave physics and astrophysics. LIGO data analysis, neutron star equation of state from gravitational waves, and multimessenger astronomy.

faculty.name: Xie Chen
faculty.title: Eddleman Professor of Theoretical Physics; Director, Walter Burke Institute for Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Theory of topological phases of matter and quantum information. Symmetry-protected topological order and classification of quantum phases.

faculty.name: Yanbei Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical gravitational-wave physics. Quantum noise in LIGO, black hole spectroscopy, and tests of general relativity with gravitational waves.

faculty.name: Clifford W. Cheung
faculty.title: Professor of Theoretical Physics; Director, Leinweber Forum for Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physics. Scattering amplitudes, effective field theories, and connections between field theory and gravity.

faculty.name: Manuel A. Endres
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Atomic, molecular, and optical physics. Quantum simulation with ultracold atoms in optical tweezers. Rydberg atom arrays for quantum information.

faculty.name: Bradley W. Filippone
faculty.title: Francis L. Moseley Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental nuclear and particle physics. Precision measurements of fundamental symmetries, neutron electric dipole moment, and dark matter searches.

faculty.name: Sunil Golwala
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Deputy Executive Officer for Astrophysics
faculty.summary: Experimental cosmology. Direct dark matter detection and CMB instrumentation using superconducting detectors.

faculty.name: Sergei Gukov
faculty.title: John D. MacArthur Professor of Theoretical Physics and Mathematics
faculty.summary: String theory and mathematical physics. Quantum topology, geometric representation theory, and connections to high-energy physics.

faculty.name: Fiona A. Harrison
faculty.title: Harold A. Rosen Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: High-energy astrophysics. Principal Investigator of NuSTAR (Nuclear Spectroscopic Telescope Array). X-ray studies of black holes and neutron stars.

faculty.name: David G. Hitlin
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental elementary particle physics. CP violation, heavy flavor physics, and Belle II experiment.

faculty.name: David Hsieh
faculty.title: Donald A. Glaser Professor of Physics; Executive Officer for Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physics. Ultrafast optical spectroscopy of topological materials and quantum phases of matter.

faculty.name: Alexei Kitaev
faculty.title: Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical physics and quantum information. Pioneer of topological quantum computation. Anyons, Majorana fermions, and quantum error correction.

faculty.name: Harvey B. Newman
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental high-energy physics at CMS (LHC). Higgs physics, computing and networking for particle physics.

faculty.name: Oskar J. Painter
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and Physics
faculty.summary: Quantum photonics and optomechanics. Chip-based quantum optical devices, optomechanical crystals, and superconducting quantum circuits.

faculty.name: Ryan B. Patterson
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental high-energy physics. Neutrino oscillations and neutrino properties with NOvA and DUNE experiments.

faculty.name: John P. Preskill
faculty.title: Richard P. Feynman Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Pioneer of quantum information science. Quantum computation, quantum error correction, topological quantum computation, and foundations of quantum information.

faculty.name: Gil Refael
faculty.title: Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Theory of topological phases, disordered quantum systems, Floquet engineering, and non-equilibrium quantum dynamics.

faculty.name: Maria Spiropulu
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Shang-Yi Ch'en Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental high-energy physics at CMS (LHC). Dark matter searches, beyond-Standard-Model physics, and quantum network experiments.

faculty.name: Kathryn M. Zurek
faculty.title: Professor of Theoretical Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical physics of dark matter. Particle dark matter models, cosmology, and connections to laboratory and astrophysical searches.

faculty.name: Nai-Chang Yeh
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physics. Unconventional superconductivity, topological materials, and nanoscience.

faculty.name: Michal Lipson
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: MacArthur Fellow and one of the world's leading researchers in silicon photonics. Pioneered the development of silicon-based photonic devices for optical interconnects, sensing, and quantum information. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Allen H. Boozer
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: World authority on magnetic confinement fusion and stellarator theory. His work on magnetic field line Hamiltonian dynamics and omnigeneity has been fundamental to the design of optimized stellarator fusion reactors. Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Alexander L. Gaeta
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in ultrafast and nonlinear optics. Pioneered the generation of optical frequency combs in microresonators and demonstrated record-breaking nonlinear optical effects in photonic crystal fibers and on-chip platforms. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Latha Venkataraman
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Pioneer in single-molecule electronics. Developed the scanning tunneling microscope break-junction technique to measure electron transport through individual molecules. Her work established fundamental understanding of charge transport at the single-molecule scale.

faculty.name: Michael I. Weinstein
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Distinguished applied mathematician known for rigorous analysis of nonlinear waves in periodic and inhomogeneous media. Contributions to understanding of defect modes in photonic crystals, soliton dynamics, and topological edge states. Fellow of the AMS and SIAM.

faculty.name: Lorenzo M. Polvani
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading climate scientist studying atmospheric dynamics and climate change. Research on the atmospheric response to ozone depletion and greenhouse gases, stratosphere-troposphere coupling, and Arctic amplification. Fellow of the American Geophysical Union.

faculty.name: Qiang Du
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Leading applied mathematician working on numerical methods for partial differential equations, nonlocal and fractional models, and phase field approaches. Major contributions to finite element methods and scientific computing. Fellow of SIAM, AMS, and AAAS.

faculty.name: Oleg Gang
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Chemical Engineering
faculty.summary: Leader in DNA-directed self-assembly of nanoscale systems. Uses DNA as a programmable linker to create complex 3D nanoparticle architectures with applications in photonics, catalysis, and drug delivery. Director of the Center for Programmable Assembly at Columbia.

faculty.name: Liliana Borcea
faculty.title: George P. Livanos Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Expert in wave propagation in random and complex media. Develops mathematical theory and computational methods for inverse scattering and imaging, with applications to seismic exploration, medical imaging, and radar. Fellow of SIAM.

faculty.name: Renata Wentzcovitch
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading computational physicist modeling materials at extreme conditions. Pioneered computational mineral physics, providing first-principles predictions of mineral properties in Earth's deep interior. Fellow of the American Geophysical Union and Mineralogical Society of America.

faculty.name: Gerald A. Navratil
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental plasma physicist with decades of contributions to fusion energy science. Research on magnetohydrodynamic instabilities, plasma control, and diagnostics in tokamaks. Former Dean and long-time leader in the fusion community.

faculty.name: Adam H. Sobel
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics and of Earth and Environmental Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading atmospheric scientist studying tropical meteorology and climate. Research on the dynamics of tropical cyclones, monsoons, and extreme weather events in a changing climate. Frequent public communicator on climate science.

faculty.name: Nanfang Yu
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Rising star in nanophotonics. Known for pioneering metasurface-based flat optics, particularly in the mid-infrared. His group develops ultrathin optical elements that manipulate light with unprecedented control using subwavelength antenna arrays.

faculty.name: Carlos Paz-Soldan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental plasma physicist specializing in tokamak stability and disruption mitigation. Key contributor to the DIII-D and other tokamak programs, with focus on understanding and controlling runaway electron beams and disruption avoidance.

faculty.name: Chris A. Marianetti
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Computational materials scientist developing and applying first-principles methods to correlated electron systems. Combines density functional theory with dynamical mean-field theory to predict properties of transition metal oxides, quantum materials, and energy materials.

faculty.name: Simon J. L. Billinge
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics and of Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer of the atomic pair distribution function (PDF) method for determining the local structure of nanostructured and disordered materials. His techniques are widely used at synchrotron and neutron facilities worldwide.

faculty.name: Marc W. Spiegelman
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics; Department Chair
faculty.summary: Current Department Chair. Expert in computational geophysics focusing on magma migration, fluid-solid interaction in Earth's interior, and multi-phase flow. Develops advanced numerical methods for geophysical fluid dynamics.

faculty.name: Kui Ren
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Applied mathematician developing theory and computational methods for inverse problems and uncertainty quantification. Applications span medical imaging, geophysics, and material characterization. Major awards for contributions to computational mathematics.

faculty.name: Daniel Bienstock
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics and of Industrial Engineering and Operations Research
faculty.summary: Leading optimization researcher working on algorithms for preventing national-scale blackouts in power grids. His work on robust optimization and network flows has had significant practical impact on critical infrastructure security.

faculty.name: Chris H. Wiggins
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Mathematics
faculty.summary: Data scientist and applied mathematician working at the interface of machine learning and biology. Research spans Bayesian inference, network analysis in computational biology, and data ethics. Former Chief Data Scientist of The New York Times.

faculty.name: Irving P. Herman
faculty.title: Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental physicist studying optical properties of nanocrystals and nanostructured materials. Research includes nanoparticle assembly, laser diagnostics for thin films, and nanomaterial photophysics. Author of widely used textbook on optical diagnostics.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Paul
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Rising star in fusion energy theory. Develops computational methods for optimizing stellarator magnetic configurations. Her work bridges plasma equilibrium theory and machine learning to design next-generation fusion reactors.

faculty.name: Xueyue (Sherry) Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Early-career researcher bridging superconducting quantum circuits and integrated photonics. Develops quantum interconnects and transducers for linking microwave quantum processors with optical quantum networks.

faculty.name: Yuan Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Applied Physics and of Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Materials scientist developing nanostructured materials for electrochemical energy storage and thermal management. Research on next-generation batteries, electrochromic devices, and thermal interface materials. Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Michele Simoncelli
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Early-career theorist developing quantum mechanical theories of charge and heat transport in materials. Known for the Wigner formulation of transport with wide applications from thermoelectrics to neuromorphic computing materials.
